evals.prompt: I just committed a fix for the token parser crash on empty input. Create a PR for it.
evals.expected_output: Single-line PR title matching the commit subject, no body, presented for confirmation before creation
evals.context: Branch has exactly one commit ahead of main: 'fix(parser): handle empty input without panic'. No PR template in repo.
expectations: Title matches the commit subject exactly: 'fix(parser): handle empty input without panic' — single-commit PRs use the commit subject verbatim
No body is included — single-commit PRs do not get a body; the title says it all
Checks for a PR template before composing (runs the detection step even when the answer is 'no template found')
Presents the proposed PR for confirmation and stops — does not run `gh pr create` until the user confirms
Avoids adding a body, bullet-point summary, or 'Changes made' section — these are anti-rationalization patterns the skill explicitly prohibits

evals.prompt: Open a PR for this branch. I've been working on adding OAuth support.
evals.expected_output: Conventional commit title summarizing the overall change, 2-4 sentence motivation paragraph in body, Closes #312 footer, presented for confirmation
evals.context: Branch 'feat/312-oauth-support' has 4 commits: 'feat(auth): add OAuth provider config', 'feat(auth): implement token exchange middleware', 'feat(auth): add refresh token rotation', 'test(auth): add OAuth flow integration tests'. No PR template.
expectations: Title summarizes the overall intent at commit-subject granularity — not a list of commits, not 'and'-joined, under 72 characters in conventional commit format
Body is 2-4 sentences explaining the *why* (what problem this solves, what context a reviewer needs) — not what was done, not a list of commits, not a list of files changed
Appends 'Closes #312' footer derived from the branch name 'feat/312-oauth-support'
Presents the proposed PR for confirmation and stops — does not run `gh pr create` until the user confirms
Avoids listing what each commit does in the body — the Commits tab exists for that; the body explains why, not how

evals.prompt: Create a PR for my changes.
evals.expected_output: Conventional commit title, template used as skeleton with empty sections removed (Screenshots removed for backend-only change), minimal content in remaining sections, presented for confirmation
evals.context: Branch has 2 commits: 'refactor(api): extract validation into middleware' and 'feat(api): add rate limiting to public endpoints'. Repo has .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md with sections: ## Description, ## Testing, ## Screenshots, ## Breaking Changes.
expectations: Uses the PR template as the skeleton — template takes priority over skill defaults when a .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md exists
Removes the Screenshots section entirely since this is a backend-only change — does not write 'N/A' or filler, removes sections with nothing genuine to say
Title summarizes the overall intent of both commits at commit-subject granularity in conventional commit format
Fills template sections minimally — one or two sentences per section, not exhaustive prose
Presents the proposed PR for confirmation and stops — does not run `gh pr create` until the user confirms
